reviews.review_text: Good game but ABSOLUTELY despise having to log in to XBOX LIVE everytime and for whatever reason, it keeps making me change passwords. Garbage of a system.

[h1] The best apocalypse community simulator, but keep your nerves ready for the bugs. [/h1]

✅ [b] State of Decay 2 (Juggernaut Edition) is exactly what you want from a zombie survival game [/b] ,building a base, managing survivors, and going on dangerous supply runs. The fear of [strike] permanent death [/strike] for your favorite character gives every choice real weight. Every car trip feels like an adventure, and the community management actually makes you think. 

❌ However, it definitely has its flaws. Even after all these years, the game is still quite [i] "janky" [/i] wonky car physics, survivors getting stuck in textures, or [u] your AI companions being absolutely brain dead [/u] , standing still while a Juggernaut rips them apart. It can also get a bit repetitive (clear house, grab rucksack, drive home, repeat). But if you’re looking for a game where your community truly matters, there isn't anything better.
This game was great. I loved the community-building and scavenging side or the zombie apocalypse, as well as the emergent storytelling. Can't wait for State of Decay 3.

Maybe I'd recommend this game as a single player, after all it seems well built and fun. But I bought this game to play co-op with my wife and the multiplayer SUCKS ASS, and therefor I'm downvoting it. 

Lets hope state of Decay 3 has a proper multiplayer exp where my wife can fully interact with my base as if it was hers as well.

State of Decay 2: Juggernaut Edition is a solid zombie survival game with enjoyable gameplay mechanics and satisfying base management. The combat, scavenging, and community-building systems work well together and keep the experience engaging.

For me, it's around a 12–15 hour game before things start to feel repetitive, but those hours are entertaining and well worth the time. If you can grab it on sale, it's an easy recommendation for fans of zombie survival games.

despite the idiotic ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ of xbox live, it is a good game...
it still has issues with areas that you would think should be able to be cleaned out and used, but no we cannot have that...

at least they have fixed all the insane bugs, only some invisible walls issues now.
(one thing this game NEEDS is mod support)

A great Zombie game with a fun gameplay loop. Manage your base, scrounge for supplies, level up your skills with different characters. Had a lot of fun with this game. Also fun with friends.

Aside from some annoying but infrequent bugs like followers getting stuck or not being able to recruit them in your base the game runs very stable and never crashed once for me.

♥♥♥♥ game
why the ♥♥♥♥ do i have to put in my microsoft creds to play the game i payed for
best zombie game dare i say-- (wwz, l4d, project zomboid, twd, tlous, you're all fading... jkjk) (wish the multiplayer did a little more but still fun) need the third one to release asap--

The game is fun, until everything starts to piss you off.
The characters are brainless and have the corniest dialogue imaginable, which constantly repeats. The game has hundreds of bugs, which the devs don't bother to fix, because they've abandoned the game. The skills and perks system could be good, if you could get rid of negative perks and useless skills, and if you could improve the skills further than 2 tiers. The ferals are a nightmare to deal with and are insanely overpowered. You can disable the special zombies, but it disables ALL of the special zombies, not just the ferals, then the game is just boring. Zombies are constantly respawning, and they respawn faster as you play the game for longer. For example, you kill a zombie, walk 7 feet away, then you turn around and it's already respawned. You need an xbox account to play this dog sh*t game, despite having bought it on steam. I'm playing heartland and trying to get my money's worth, but I just had 2 juggernauts, 4 screamers, a feral, and 3 hordes spawn outside my base. One of the hordes and a screamer were stuck inside a boulder. You have to deal with all of that crap, on top of having quest after quest shoveled on top of you, and if you don't complete the quest in time, they get mad at you. You can't expand your base, only move to bigger ones. You can't add more building slots in your base either. Update: finished heartland and it was one of the most underwhelming endings to a game I've ever seen. My mistake for expecting anything good to come out of this dogsh*t
One of my favorite games. The way the developers continued to work on this games for ages after release was amazing.
It's a good sandbox game, very polished, very pleasant, the multiplayer kinds sucks, but the gamepaly it's solid

I do really like this game, but with one major caveat: It's buggy as hell. Just as buggy as when it was first released (despite supposed "bug fixes"). What kinds of bugs, you ask? Well:
- Characters and zombies getting stuck in geometry (or disappearing entirely).
- Characters being unable to be played because they're "busy" (even when they aren't doing anything and aren't in the infirmary...this happens CONSTANTLY). You have to actually quit and reload the game to fix this.
- Characters not going to the infirmary to get cured from plague (and also being unable to be located in your base).
- Vehicles randomly crashing into invisible walls.

I love you, Undead Labs, but my God...this game has been out for 8 years and is still as janky as it was on day 1.
Peak zombie game, get maximum cortisol level when chased by 3 blood plague ferals
State of Decay 2 remains one of the best zombie survival sandbox experiences available, especially with friends in co-op!

On paper, I should not be enjoying this game as much as I do. There's not much of a plot, all characters are Animal Crossing-like with the pre-determined voice lines that only vaguely interacts with other characters, and there's not much exploration other than the constant looting of little spots on the map.

But this game is incredibly addicting. The gameplay loop and management system is so satisfying. It's a perfect mix of survival, looting, community management, and base-building. The random missions, plague hearts, curveballs, and other enclaves makes it so that you never run out of missions to do. And when you DO eventually finish all the missions, there's hours upon hours of lootable places to visit. And when you finish all of that, there's other maps to do it all over again.

 What I enjoy about this game is that it's an amazing time-killer without the need to fully commit because the focus of the game is the looting and base-management. I dropped this game for a year and only got back recently and I felt like I didn't leave because there was no need to get caught up with the story again and I only needed to jump back into the action. It's great. Get this game. Can't wait for the next game.
The mystery that surrounds this blood plague intrigues me.  I find myself drawn to this game everytime I choose to play.
was a fun mindless game when u want something to do, cant wait for state of decay 3 though multiplayer and maybe better bases . hopefully bigger open world and more characters  with better ai

One of the best zombie survival games I’ve played recently after playing Days Gone. The only downfall of the game is the online multiplayer limitation, but they are improving that in State of Decay 3.

The permadeath system in State of Decay 2 makes me feel real fear when I play with my friends or even alone on the highest difficulty. I literally scream, “Oh no, I’m dying, dying!” because I keep thinking about whether my favorite character will die or not — especially when I see a 😒 Feral on the max difficulty.

Sometimes, when I’m playing in my friend’s multiplayer session, I secretly exit back to my own game when I’m low on bullets or infected with Blood Plague 😁… that’s all.
I'm waiting for state of decay 3 while still playing SOD 2. i've already done a lot in this game, yet still a lot lot more to explore. 10/10

3rd person base management with zombie cluster clearing. 
It's pretty fun, but it does get repetitive after the first map is complete. You can go on to finish the others if your itch still needs scratching but it doesn't really feel like you are building up to an achievement of any sort. 
Still a thumbs up. Good, could be a bit better.

this is Classic Zombie/Base Building/smashing zombies with car doors kind  of fun. One I return to on a constant basis. Though they stopped supporting this one in favor of  3.  I find the adventure challenging and fun.
It's good. For m&k it can be a little annoying with menus and the like. It's a more controller-intuitive design. But overall it's a fun game.
its fun, I always liked the idea of a singleplayer open world zombie RPG game
Fantastic zombie game, in higher difficulties the special spawn rate is a bit insane but still always a black for each land you take back.

To be honest I am not the biggest fan of zombies in general. I think the idea of them got old over the years. However, this game is definitely fun. 

While finnicky and sometimes lacking explanation, the base system in this game is glorious, albeit kinda limited. 

The game makes up for that by providing the player with a huge open world and cool vehicles.

The game is really good for a lot of reasons. One being it's both an easy and hard game to play. It has easy modes for people who are new to the game or just want to relax and it has a mode that makes it challenging beyond belief. Other than that, there are still some bugs here and there but every game is going to have bugs. I do like that there is a radio command in the game that gets you unstuck and it costs zero points to use, pretty handy feature. I think my biggest gripe about the game is the multiplayer that has to do with tethering. I wish we could all just roam around the whole map and scavenge other places so that we could be even more helpful for the people we are on the server with. Overall though, great game, beautiful and doesn't have any issues with lag at all. Very great game and 100% an upgrade from the first game.

Its an amazing game for it's freedom and immersion on being a couple of survivors trying to make a safe colony together, but i uninstalled the game out of frustration because it continues to spawn zombies out of thin air which kept disrupting me, ruining my stealth and made me lose my 8 hour run.

The game has a lot going for it but I really do recommend playing this game in a difficulty easier than nightmare because its not fun to spend hours on trying to do an objective because zombies just never stop coming. You will spend about 10 minutes clearing out an area only to make the game send three ferals at you when they weren't even visible in the map before you decided to explore outside your base.

The looting, map and exploration is amazing, and I love getting power spikes as your characters gain new skills, but holy crap the most unenjoyable aspect in this game are the zombies themselves because they just keep spawning more than what you can manage.

This would not be a bad thing if you had the ability to clear hordes, dead rising did it and the game teaches you that you just need to fight through the horde to reach your objectives, but special zombies and zombies that are grouped together do not despawn and they eventually accumulate on your minimap and dealing with them is inevitable. Guns and explosives are scarce, the melees lack any crowd control and their clunkiness of hitting one zombie at a time do not help.

They gave special zombies permanence, but regular zombies spawn all the time, you can snipe every zombie in an open wheat field, be aware the coast is clear and still get jumped by a zombie from behind after a few seconds, its infuriating and tedious.

I look forward to the third game if it gets proper multiplayer with shared progress in one world, as this game has great potential in playing with friends as managing a colony by yourself is honestly stressful if a zombie approaches you every 8 seconds on average

Great game even after all these years, has a few issues like hdr going funny depending on displays but easy fix worth the buy. needs xbox liove acount linked to play another downside if your not into that.
Almost a perfect game, but the technical issues will constantly rob you of hours of progress. Just like real life... *sobs quietly*

On top of that I cant interact with them when im away from base with them and they lose all their inventory backpack and all.

[h1]Disclaimer[/h1]
I finished this game several times, clocking over 200h total, some of it on Gamepass back when it was priced reasonably.

[h1]TL;DR[/h1]
Fantastic.

It's a stellar example of a game that's more than a sum of its parts.

Considering it goes on sale frequently and doesn't cost much at all - I believe you should give it a try.

[h1]Description[/h1]
This is a large game containing many systems that interact with each other. At its core it's a zombie survival game with colony management. I will try to describe parts of it clearly, but quickly.

General gameplay:
You will take control of one of your group members (which you can switch at base). Each person has skills in cardio, fighting, shooting and wits, some have additional skills that unlock special actions/facilities like mechanics or medicine OR they can have special passive bonus skills like music. You then set out into the world to find supplies, resources and complete tasks that grant influence. Utilizing each skill improves it, so fighting zombies with a melee weapon improves fighting. You can use cars to travel, but they are noisy. Improving skills will let you choose a specialisation from a list that depends on survivor traits. For example - fighting may be improved to swordfighting or striking. Special skills like medicine also get specialisations. Survivor that does enough tasks that bring influence will eventually advance to citizen or hero - giving passive bonuses to the entire group.

Combat:
It's based on soft-lock system. Each time you swing you lock onto an enemy. You can also dodge and finish off knocked down or unalerted zombies. Shooting works as you'd expect.

Base management:
You can choose from several build sites, each offering different advantages and cost. Each base will have a number of pre-built facilities and open slots indoors or outdoors. Slots may be large or small. You have 5 resources: food, medicine, materials, ammo and fuel. Depending on your base upgrades, some resources are used per day in addition to food which is consumed by your group. Beds are needed to keep people happy. Facilities like workshop let you repair or make weapons and grenades, and infirmary helps wounded recover. You can also treat infection. There are special facilities unlocked by having specific skills like automechanic letting you build an auto-shop that in turn lets you create vehicle upgrades. Bases create noise that makes more zombies spawn around its perimeter. Your base will have a leader of one out of 4 classes: sheriff, warlord, trader or builder, each unlocking different upgrades or bonuses. Once you clear the game as a specific leader - you also unlock a meta-upgrade for future playthrough based on your leader in all difficulties up to the one you finished on. Warlord leader gives you some good starting guns for example. You can also build outposts that provide you with power, water or a daily resource income.

Zombies:
There are 2 basic varieties - normal and red-skinned plague infected. Red ones will apply infection when hitting you. If you get enough infection, you will become fully infected and have a timer that will kill you unless you get cured. There are also special zombies. Screamers alert more enemies if they see you. Bloaters explode when killed, releasing gas that makes you take trauma and damage. Freaks run quick and attack relentlessly, but can be headshot dead. And finally - juggernauts which are huge and take lots of punishment and dish out even more. Zombies may execute you if you have low enough HP and you allow them to grab you, killing you instantly, so be careful.

Goal:
While you will get different missions from friendly survivor groups that reward influence you can then use for favors or trade - the main goal is to clear the map of plague hearts. These hearts are source of the plague and killing them requires preparation and commitment as when they are attacked - they alert all zombies in large area to save them. Once you finish the heart - it drops some quality items too. Once you kill all hearts, you need to finish some last quests from your leader in order to finish the map. You can then continue on current map to clear it fully, go on to a different map with your current group or make a new game entirely. Awakened plague hearts if not dealt with fully - will start sending zombies to siege your base periodically, forcing you to go back and defend.

[h1]Pros:[/h1]
[b]+ Well designed[/b]
The way each system interacts with the larger whole is nothing short of brilliant. I don't think I've ever seen a game like that that's not a 2D top-down management sim. This is an action game, but has so much going for it.

Even a simple act of looking for resources comes with tons of questions and strategies. Go by car to have more room for items and cover more ground, or go on foot which is quieter?

[b]+ Tons of choices and strategies[/b]
I recommend to really go through each building and what it gives you. Radio can give you 20% boost to influence gains at the cost of 1 guy operating it. Unneeded weapons can be dismantled for parts to repair others or make something else. Choosing survivors with special skills may be beneficial in the short run, but having ones with no skills means you can teach them whatever you need.

[b]+ Genuine survival[/b]
Aside from reliance on obtaining resources - you also need to deal with plague hearts. If you kill too many of their plague zombies, they will start sending hordes after you. Getting hit may lead to deep wounds that decrease your HP and even infection. Infected survivors can be cured with some medicine and plague samples, but it's better to avoid such problems to begin with. Shooting guns or driving a car means noise and you need a plan on how to deal with whatever may come your way.

[b]+ Mods make it even better[/b]
I usually don't mention mods, but here I believe you can genuinely make the game greater with mods. Improved car HP/fuel economy and more slots in the trunk is one thing that I just don't think you should play without at all. It gets quite unreasonable when the car can hold about as much as a backpack.

[h1]Cons:[/h1]
[b]- Jank[/b]
Unfortunately there's a lot of it.

The biggest culprit is melee combat, which relies on a soft-lockon which sometimes fails. When you are in a hurry, some button inputs may be dropped, like dodging and immediately trying to vault an obstacle.

[b]- Cars 'balance'[/b]
Cars are waaaaaay too fragile. Some cars get like 10% off their HP after hitting 1 zombie, like come on.

[b]- Tedium[/b]
Eventually this game may become tedious. Going door-to-door, searching for stuff and going back home to deposit is fun at first, gets repetitive later. It will take you probably more than 25h on your first playthrough to finish the game and over that period of time - the simplicity of different mechanics can really show.

Please note that this complaint comes from someone who played this game through 4 times, clocking probably around 200h already. But the game is relatively simple in each mechanic, it's the combination of them that gives it depth.

[b]- DEI[/b]
Since this is a microsoft published game - obviously it adheres to DEI. In my survivor groups majority are always black, seems to be that it's the most common skin color on survivors. No clear Asians. Zombies tend to be white though. Interesting.

Women are often old or unattractive.

Not a huge complaint, but it's annoying that seemingly every game nowadays just cannot have conventionally attractive women and everything needs to be diverse save for enemies.

You could technically exile all colored survivors... you know... if you want to do that... I couldn't be bothered.

[h1]Lowdown[/h1]
I can't believe how they took everything that was good about SoD1 and improved on it in the sequel.

I'd give State of Decay 2 a
[h1]9/10[/h1]
I hope they will improve the sequel.

Not just no, but hell no.  I can't recommend a game that is actually incomplete and rushed just to make money. Turn around and then look back, and see the inner parts of a truck, and my favorite, falling through the actual ground into oblivion.

Got it on sale for incredibly low price (~£1-3 or something)

...Even then, wouldn't recommend. Waste of money. you get what you pay for. Got bored within minutes. Big gripes with the game:

> Shallow unengaging gameplay/base building.
> No character customization - the game locks you into pre-built characters that are liekwise lacking depth or engagement. They are literally so boring and it isn't remotely relavent to the gameplay.
> Virtually no story.
> Instead of actual missions, randomly semi-generated missions. That's the entire game. 
>Lack of combat complexity. There are two buttons: Left click to attack. Right click to shoot.
> Lack of interesting enemies. There are probably only 5 types of enemy in the game, regular, crawler, screamer, juggernaut, bloater, from what I can see? You'll mainly see just the regular, crawler, and screamer occasionally. They are all laughably-easy to defeat. You right click to shoot a screamer, run away, come back and crouch + walk around stealth killing all the enemies. If you need to, you can just run over them endlessly with the car.
> Overpowered stealth. The stealth in the game is ridiculously broken. You can crouch right next to a zombie at it's sideline and it won't even see you. Zombies generally in this game have ridiculously poor detection capability.
> Annoying npcs. The NPC will constantly run circles around you, and more often than not will alert a screamer or get you killed. They're only minorly helpful by running missions off-screen (that you never ask them to do!) which honestly removes part of the fun of the game, scavenging for supplies. Npc's generally are just not useful because why take an npcc when you can just take the car?
> Lack of base building flexibility. As mentioned, the base structure is very rigid and not at all very immersive. 
> Simplistic crafting system. The crafting system is very simple and generic, using generic resources to build pretty much all of the stuff in the game. This isn't very immersive when the gameplay becomes "just gather generic scrap resource".

If you want an actually engaging zombie survival community basebuilding game:
> My advice, try Project zomboid, Minecraft zombie mods, or other titles. A good alternative for SoD2 that players from the genre have reccomended is Dead island.

>Also, the game may be considered abandonware, as the game contained all of these faults on release - after several years, there has been virtually nothing done by the devs to address this.

>Developers often list poorly released titles with bad reviews on massive sale to scrape by some revenue. This isn't limited to Undead labs, many developers do this. My advice is don't fall for it: It's not worth it. It is tempting. Save your money for a quality game title.

The game was very fun and promising in the beginning but once I realised the multiplayer only benefits the host, that's where everything went downhill...
When you play multiplayer and connect to the person that hosts the game, you quickly realise that the host has most power of the whole game. Everything you build in the base, all survivors that you free, all facilities, resources and progession ONLY counts for the host. What exactly is the point for the guests to play with a host if only he gets everything?? Very disappointing unfortunately..
I like the gameplay loop, gives a satisfying set of needs to complete.  Being able to change maps with a kitted out crew, and even then needing to really plan your moves is a great plus.
